books.title: Reading American Horizons
books.author: Various Authors
books.rationale: From sources like Andrew Jackson’s “Veto of the Bank of the United States" to “the Declaration of Sentiments,” students will get their hands on written and visual documents that will help them see firsthand what people in the 1800s were encountering on a day-to-day basis. Students will develop their historical thinking skills by using these primary sources.

books.title: The Narrative of Lunsford Lane, Formerly of Raleigh, N. C.
books.author: Lunsford Lane
books.rationale: Originally published in 1842, this autobiography of Lunsford Lane details his life of slavery and his eventual escape using a unique method, purchasing his own freedom. To do this, he would need to have help from others around him. Lunsford Lane faced many hardships and took on entire judicial systems in his quest for his and his family’s freedom. Students, when reading The Narrative of Lunsford Lane, will encounter the real-life story of a brave man who lost his home but freed himself and his family.

books.title: American History, Volume 1: 1492-1877
books.author: Thomas Kidd
books.rationale: In American History: Volumes 1 and 2, Thomas Kidd tells the story of the United States from early American encounters to the modern-day American culture in an engaging and fresh way. Thomas Kidd uses primary sources and other scholarly resources to introduce the student to the people and places that have great historical significance—whether good or bad.

books.title: They Fought Like Demons: Women Soldiers in the Civil War
books.author: De Anne Blanton & Lauren M. Cook
books.rationale: Most people will never know the truth behind people like Albert Cashier, Frances Clayton, or the countless other aliases that women took on so that they could join the fight for causes that they believed in. The authors dive into military archives with the goal to uncover the many stories of women who stepped into the role of a male soldier in the 1800s. And with engaging chapters and well-rounded research, the student will be able to see the war through the eyes of women as they took to the battlefield alongside brothers, sons, fathers, and husbands. These women would come to be known later on—courtesy of documents from soldiers—as soldiers who fought like demons.

books.title: American History, Volume 2: 1877 - Present
books.author: Thomas Kidd
books.rationale: In American History: Volumes 1 and 2, Thomas Kidd tells the story of the United States from early American encounters to the modern-day American culture in an engaging and fresh way. Thomas Kidd uses primary sources and other scholarly resources to introduce the student to the people and places that have great historical significance—whether good or bad.

books.title: I Am a Man: Chief Standing Bear's Journey for Justice
books.author: Joe Starita
books.rationale: This book is about Chief Standing Bear’s fight for justice as he navigates the loss of his land—taken by manipulation and coercion. Joe Starita uses primary sources to help the audience see many of the hardships that Chief Standing Bear went through — like the loss of his daughter and brother —and how others came to fight for him and his people. It grounds the reader in the time and experiences of another people group who were struggling to find their identity in a new nation.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
